text.title: django-base-model Procedure
text.summary: Six-step procedure to convert an ERD into a validated base-model spec.

step.name: declare-bases
step.action: Declare BaseModel (always: TimestampMixin + UidMixin) and SoftDeletableModel (BaseModel + SoftDeleteMixin) and, if multi-tenant, TenantAwareModel.

step.name: route-models
step.input: List of concrete models from ERD.
step.action: For each concrete model: pick extends ∈ {BaseModel, SoftDeletableModel, TenantAwareModel}; set soft_delete + exposes_uid + tenant_scoped flags accordingly.
step.output: models array with extends field set.

step.name: enumerate-unique-fields
step.action: For every soft-deletable model, list unique fields. They MUST be implemented as partial UniqueConstraints; document in the spec consumer's migration notes.
step.decision-gate: Reject any soft-deletable model that retains plain `unique=True` declarations.

step.name: audit-foreign-keys
step.action: For every FK, name on_delete explicitly with a `reason` >= 10 chars. Default-CASCADE without a reason is rejected.
step.decision-gate: If on_delete=CASCADE and the parent model has soft_delete=true, require reason naming explicit cascade intent.

step.name: declare-tenant-context
step.action: For each tenant-scoped model, document that Celery / management-command access must wrap in tenant_context(tenant) per rule r7-tenant-context.
